015 * under the License.
016 */
017
018package org.tquadrat.foundation.svg.type;
019
020import static java.lang.Math.abs;
021import static java.lang.String.format;
022import static org.apiguardian.api.API.Status.STABLE;
023import static org.tquadrat.foundation.lang.Objects.requireNotEmptyArgument;
024
025import java.util.function.IntUnaryOperator;
026
027import org.apiguardian.api.API;
028import org.tquadrat.foundation.annotation.ClassVersion;
029
030/**
031 *  The implementation for SVG values representing colors.
032 *
033 *  @extauthor Thomas Thrien - thomas.thrien@tquadrat.org
034 *  @version $Id: SVGColor.java 1151 2025-10-01 21:32:15Z tquadrat $
035 *  @since 0.0.5
036 *
037 *  @UMLGraph.link
038 */
039@SuppressWarnings( "EqualsAndHashcode" )
040@ClassVersion( sourceVersion = "$Id: SVGColor.java 1151 2025-10-01 21:32:15Z tquadrat $" )
041@API( status = STABLE, since = "0.0.5" )
042public final class SVGColor extends SVGPaint
043{
044        /*-----------*\
045    ====** Constants **========================================================
046        \*-----------*/
047    /**
048     *  This constant is used to force the colour values into the range from
049     *  0 to 255.
050     */
051    private static final int m_Divisor = 0x100;
052
053        /*------------------------*\
054    ====** Static Initialisations **===========================================
055        \*------------------------*/
056    /**
057     *  The type 'inherit'.
058     */
059    public static final SVGColor COLOR_INHERIT;
060
061    static
062    {
063        COLOR_INHERIT = new SVGColor();
064    }
065
066        /*--------------*\
067    ====** Constructors **=====================================================
068        \*--------------*/
069    /**
070     *  Creates a new {@code SVGColor} instance with the type
071     *  &quot;inherit&quot;.
072     */
073    private SVGColor() { super( "inherit" ); }
074
075    /**
076     *  <p>{@summary Creates a new {@code SVGColor} instance from the given
077     *  colour values.}</p>
078     *  <p>Valid values are in the range from 0 to 255, other values will be
079     *  normalised accordingly.</p>
080     *
081     *  @param  red The red component for the colour.
082     *  @param  green   The green component for the colour.
083     *  @param  blue    The blue component for the colour.
084     */
085    public SVGColor( final int red, final int green, final int blue )
086    {
087        super( format( "#%02x%02x%02x", abs( red ) % m_Divisor, abs( green ) % m_Divisor, abs( blue ) % m_Divisor ) );
088    }   //  SVGColor()
089
090    /**
091     *  <p>{@summary Creates a new {@code SVGColor} instance from the given
092     *  colour values}.</p>
093     *  <p>Valid values are in the range from 0 to 255, or from 0% to 100%
094     *  respectively, other values will be normalised accordingly.</p>
095     *
096     *  @param  flag    {@code true} if the given values are percentages,
097     *      {@code false} if they are absolute values.
098     *  @param  red The red component for the colour.
099     *  @param  green   The green component for the colour.
100     *  @param  blue    The blue component for the colour.
101     */
102    public SVGColor( final boolean flag, final int red, final int green, final int blue )
103    {
104        super( composeColorValue( flag, red, green, blue ) );
105    }   //  SVGColor()
106
107    /**
108     *  <p>{@summary Creates a new {@code SVGColor} instance, using the given
109     *  argument as a CSS colour name.}</p>
110     *  <p>The given argument may not be {@code null} nor the empty String,
111     *  but it will not undergo any further validation.</p>
112     *
113     *  @param  color   The CSS colour name.
114     */
115    public SVGColor( final String color ) { super( requireNotEmptyArgument( color, "color" ) ); }
116
117        /*---------*\
118    ====** Methods **==========================================================
119        \*---------*/
120    /**
121     *  <p>{@summary Composes the colour type String from the given colour
122     *  values.}</p>
123     *  <p>Valid values are in the range from 0 to 255, or from 0% to 100%
124     *  respectively, other values will be normalised accordingly.</p>
125     *
126     *  @param  flag    {@code true} if the given values are percentages,
127     *      {@code false} if they are absolute values.
128     *  @param  red The red component for the colour.
129     *  @param  green   The green component for the colour.
130     *  @param  blue    The blue component for the colour.
131     *  @return The colour type String.
132     */
133    private static final String composeColorValue( final boolean flag, final int red, final int green, final int blue )
134    {
135        final var convertToPercentage = (IntUnaryOperator) v -> abs( v ) > 100 ? abs( v ) % 101 : abs( v );
136        final var retValue = flag
137            ? format( "rgb(%d%%,%d%%,%d%%)", convertToPercentage.applyAsInt( red ), convertToPercentage.applyAsInt( green ), convertToPercentage.applyAsInt( blue ) )
138            : format( "rgb(%d,%d,%d)", abs( red ) % m_Divisor, abs( green ) % m_Divisor, abs( blue ) % m_Divisor );
139
140        //---* Done *----------------------------------------------------------
141        return retValue;
142    }   //  composeColorValue()
143
144    /**
145     *  {@inheritDoc}
146     */
147    @Override
148    public final boolean equals( final Object obj )
149    {
150        var retValue = this == obj;
151        if( !retValue && (obj instanceof final SVGColor other) )
152        {
153            retValue = value().equals( other.value() );
154        }
155
156        //---* Done *----------------------------------------------------------
157        return retValue;
158    }   //  equals()
159}
160//  class SVGColor
161
162/*
163 *  End of File
164 */
